features.label: WebRTC web API (RTCPeerConnection)

patches.author: Masayuki Nakano
patches.subject: Make `nsPIDOMWindow.h` stop including `nsPIDOMWindowInlines.h`

patches.author: Anna Weine
patches.subject: Implement getRemoteCertificates

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Remove the cache-clearing side effect of TS_NONE.

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Fire closed updates for transports that are torn down, even if they were never active.

patches.author: Philipp Hancke
patches.subject: fix webrtc-stats transportId references

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, which underpins real-time communication like video calls, have focused on improving its reliability and functionality. One notable enhancement is the implementation of the `getRemoteCertificates` method, allowing applications to access the certificates being used in peer-to-peer connections, thus enhancing security by enabling better management of encryption credentials. Additionally, updates have eliminated unnecessary cache-clearing behavior and ensured that transport connections properly notify when they are closed, improving the accuracy of connection status updates. These changes collectively mean that developers will have a more robust framework for handling video and audio communications which, in turn, can lead to **more reliable and secure user experiences** in applications that rely on WebRTC.

features.label: Connection transport (ICE / DTLS)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Pass already_AddRefed types by-value instead of by rvalue reference, in dom/{ipc,mathml,media,notification,streams}.

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 22: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in webrtc jsapi, sdp and logging bridges.

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: remove unused macros from r_macros.h

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: remove unused NR_reg_set_bytes and NR_reg_set2_string.

features.summary: Recent updates in the connection transport area of Firefox, particularly around WebRTC (the technology behind video calls), focus on improving the efficiency and clarity of the code that handles how connections are established and maintained. One significant change is the use of **_MOZ_LOG_FMT_** in the logging mechanisms for WebRTC, which will enhance the way that connection-related events are recorded and debugged, making it easier for developers to monitor and troubleshoot issues. Additionally, some unused code has been removed, helping to streamline the overall architecture and remove potential sources of confusion or error, contributing to a more reliable user experience.

features.label: Negotiation engine (JSEP)

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Don't treat missing rtx pt as an error here.

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Pay attention to config for rtx/red.

features.summary: Recent updates to the negotiation engine in Firefox have improved its handling of Real-time Transport Protocol (RTP) extensions, specifically regarding the use of the RTX (retransmission) and RED (RTP payload format for redundancy) formats. These changes ensure that **the absence of a specified RTX payload type no longer triggers an error**, providing a smoother experience in scenarios such as video conferencing where network conditions can be variable. This enhancement allows for more flexibility in how Firefox handles media streams, ultimately improving the performance and reliability of **WebRTC, the technology behind video calls**.

features.label: WebRTC

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 19: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in webrtc MOZ_LOG-based logs.

patches.author: Anna Weine
patches.subject: Update webrtcdtls.KEA expiration to FF 158

features.summary: Recent updates to Firefox's WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) have improved logging processes to enhance the debugging and performance monitoring capabilities for developers using this framework. Additionally, the expiration of WebRTC DTLS (Datagram Transport Layer Security) keys has been adjusted to align with Firefox version 158, which helps maintain security standards and keep users' communications safe. These changes ensure that developers can better understand how WebRTC is functioning while also reinforcing the security of real-time communications.

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 14: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in systemservices.

features.summary: Recent updates to the camera and audio capture features in Firefox have improved the way the browser logs events within the system services that manage these functionalities. Specifically, the integration of **MOZ_LOG_FMT** enhances the logging format to provide clearer and more detailed information for developers working on issues related to capturing audio and video. While this change primarily benefits developers by making debugging easier, it ultimately contributes to a more reliable experience for users engaging in activities like video calls or streaming, as it helps ensure that these features work smoothly and effectively.

features.label: libwebrtc integration

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 26: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in gmp.

features.summary: Recent changes in the libwebrtc integration for Firefox focus on enhancing logging capabilities by implementing a standardized logging format with **MOZ_LOG_FMT**. This improvement makes it easier for developers to track and diagnose issues related to WebRTC, the technology that enables real-time communication like video calls directly in the browser. By streamlining how logs are generated, developers can gain better insights into performance and potential bugs, ultimately leading to a more reliable experience for users _without any visible change_ in how WebRTC functions day-to-day.

features.label: Connection transport (ICE / DTLS)

features.summary: Recent updates in Firefox's connection transport area enhance the underlying technologies that support features like video calls and real-time communication through WebRTC. Key improvements include the implementation of **RTCTransportStats**, which provides vital metrics about the security aspects of connections, such as the TLS version and encryption ciphers used, helping developers better understand the security performance of their applications. Additionally, code modernization efforts, such as adopting 'MakeUnique' for memory allocation, improve overall efficiency and safety, while the organization of socket attributes streamlines how the browser manages connection information. Collectively, these changes are aimed at ensuring a more secure, efficient, and robust experience for users engaging in real-time communications online.

features.label: WebRTC web API (RTCPeerConnection)

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API have improved the handling of remote peer connections and the efficiency of memory management. Notably, the implementation of **getRemoteCertificates** enhances security by allowing access to remote certificates during the connection process, which is essential for secure communications. Additionally, transport-cc has been enabled for audio codecs, optimizing the transmission of audio data by providing feedback on network conditions. Overall, these changes contribute to a more robust and secure experience for users engaging in real-time communication, such as video calls or online meetings.

features.summary: Recent updates to Firefox's integration with **WebRTC**, the technology behind video calls, have focused on enhancing video streaming capabilities and overall performance. Notably, support for the H.264 video format has been improved by integrating a more accurate parser and refining how the browser handles media capabilities based on video resolution and frame rate. These changes also include improved logging for video data, which helps developers better diagnose issues, and a more efficient management of audio bitrate for smoother transmissions. Overall, these enhancements are aimed at ensuring a higher quality video communication experience for users, especially as demand for reliable video calls continues to grow.

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC testing framework in Firefox focused on enhancing compatibility and reliability on macOS platforms. The changes include the re-enabling of several important test types, such as mochitests for screen sharing, which is critical for ensuring the stability of video calls and other real-time communication features. Additionally, crashtests, which help identify when the browser crashes under specific conditions, were re-enabled and optimized. These adjustments are essential to maintain a smooth and reliable experience for users engaging in WebRTC activities, ensuring that technology behind **video calls and online collaboration tools** works seamlessly on newer macOS versions.

patches.subject: Activate performance-inefficient-string-concatenation clang-tidy check

features.summary: Recent changes in the negotiation engine of Firefox's WebRTC implementation, specifically in the JSEP (JavaScript Session Establishment Protocol) area, enhance the handling of media tracks during communication sessions. The updates mean that **missing certain configurations won't trigger errors**, improving connection reliability. Additionally, the system is now better at associating media transceivers only when needed, which helps streamline the negotiation process for video and audio streams. These optimizations are crucial for maintaining smooth and efficient real-time communication, making user experiences during video calls or online meetings more reliable overall.

features.label: Session negotiation (SDP)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Use MakeUnique instead of 'new' to populate UniquePtrs in dom/media/webrtc/{jsep,sdp}.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Change SdpAttributeList::SetAttribute() and its callers to transfer ownership using UniquePtr.

patches.author: Nico Grunbaum
patches.subject: lint, constify, and fix includes in SDP glue;r=dbaker

features.summary: Recent updates in the session negotiation area of Firefox, particularly concerning the Session Description Protocol (SDP) used in WebRTC (the technology behind video calls), focused on improving memory management and ownership handling. By transitioning to **UniquePtr**, a smart pointer type in C++, the code now better manages the lifecycle of SDP attributes, reducing the risk of memory leaks and improving overall stability. This change enhances the performance and reliability of features that depend on SDP for negotiating media sessions, ensuring that video and audio connections are more robust and efficient _without resulting in visible changes_ for users.

features.label: WebRTC

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) implementation in Firefox focus on enhancing logging, improving security, and refining media capabilities. The introduction of **MOZ_LOG_FMT** improves how logs are formatted, aiding developers in diagnosing issues more effectively. Additionally, updates to WebRTC's H.264 video codec include rejecting requests that exceed specified resolution or framerate limits, which helps ensure optimal video performance. Security has also been bolstered with the expiration update for webrtcdtls.KEA, ensuring that encryption keys remain secure over time. These changes collectively enhance both the reliability and efficiency of real-time communications in Firefox.

features.label: libwebrtc build

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to check for missing branch-head commits.

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to show branch-head commits for libwebrtc.

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: updated default_config_env for v149

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build have introduced new Python scripts designed to enhance the management of code versions. One script checks for any missing branch-head commits, ensuring that developers can keep track of the latest updates, while another displays the current branch-head commits for libwebrtc, simplifying version oversight. Additionally, the default configuration environment has been updated for version v149, which helps maintain compatibility with the latest development standards. These changes are important because they improve the efficiency of developers working with **libwebrtc, the underlying technology for video calls**, ensuring a smoother development process and reducing the risk of errors.

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 14: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in systemservices.

patches.author: Andreas Pehrson
patches.subject: Use EventTargetCapability throughout CamerasParent.

features.summary: Recent updates to the camera and audio capture components in Firefox focus on enhancing the logging and event handling capabilities. By implementing **MOZ_LOG_FMT in system services**, developers can now better track and diagnose issues related to camera functionality, improving overall reliability for users. Additionally, integrating **EventTargetCapability throughout CamerasParent** allows for more efficient management of camera-related events, ensuring smoother and more responsive interactions when using video. These improvements collectively enhance the user experience, particularly for applications that rely heavily on real-time audio and visual input, such as video conferencing and streaming.

features.label: Android camera capture

patches.author: Daniel Baker
patches.subject: Read camera rotation from a window context so it tracks device rotation on Android.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Android camera capture functionality in Firefox now allow the browser to automatically read the camera's rotation based on the device's orientation. This means that when you turn your device, the camera will adjust accordingly, providing a more intuitive experience when taking photos or recording videos. **This improvement enhances user convenience and ensures that images and videos are correctly aligned, mitigating issues with upside-down or sideways captures.**

features.label: Test camera capture

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Bug 2040478 (MOZ) - Rename GetRealTimeClockRaw, and update a test for a newly-populated stat (was always 0 previously).

features.summary: Recent changes in the camera capture testing area include a renaming of a function to improve clarity and alignment with the latest development standards. Additionally, updates were made to a test related to the real-time clock used in capturing video, which now features a newly populated statistic that was previously returning zero. These adjustments enhance the accuracy and reliability of camera-related functionalities, ensuring better performance for applications utilizing these features, particularly in real-time scenarios.
